mnemonics: Matrix is 2D (Rows & Columns), Array is Multi-D (Like a Data Cube)
keyPoints: A matrix is a two-dimensional data structure that can only contain elements of the same data type.
An array is a multi-dimensional extension of a matrix and can also only contain elements of a single data type.
You can create matrices with the matrix() function and arrays with the array() function, specifying data and dimensions.
commonMistakes: Trying to store data of different types (e.g., numbers and text) in a single matrix or array, which will cause the data to be coerced to a single type (often to character).
Providing incorrect dimensions or an incorrect number of elements when creating a matrix or array, leading to errors or unexpected recycling of values.
